items.title: Flood control, water security in focus as Malaysia and Thailand step up Sungai Golok cooperation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 7 — The Thai Cabinet’s approval of the Sungai Golok Basin development project opens up avenues for closer cooperation between Malaysia and Thailand in addressing flood risks, water security and ecosystem restoration in the border areas of both countries. National Security Council (NSC) Director-General Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin said Malaysia also intends to implement related developments in the area, though the matter is still in the planning stages. The Thai Cabinet was previously reported to have approved a joint environmental project with Malaysia aimed at reducing flood risks, enhancing water security and restoring the ecosystem in the Sungai Golok Basin.

items.title: MACC uncovers ghost workers, forged documents in RM9m Daya Kerjaya 2.0 claims
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 7 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has uncovered irregularities involving the government’s Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said investigations, with an operation expected to begin next week, found that 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide were linked to the alleged misuse of government funds under the Social Security Organisation’s (Perkeso) initiative in 2024 and 2025. He said the MACC had also identified another 1,638 companies suspected of engaging in similar activities involving about RM45 million, although those cases remain at a preliminary stage of investigation.

items.title: Maggi, Kemas back women entrepreneurs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maggi Malaysia has partnered with the Community Development Department (Kemas) to expand its women-focused entrepreneurship programme, particularly in rural and underserved communities. As part of the WCB 4.0 programme, women are invited to submit business ideas by June 30. Maggi Malaysia and Singapore business executive officer Ivy Tan Link Cheh said the programme aims to equip women with entrepreneurial skills while leveraging growing opportunities in the digital economy.

items.title: Jaslinda recovering well after 14-day ordeal in Perak jungle, hospital monitoring continues
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — Female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe yesterday after going missing for 14 days at Gunung Batu Putih, remains in stable condition but requires further treatment and monitoring at Tapah Hospital. Perak Health Director Datuk Dr Feisul Idzwan Mustapha said Jaslinda underwent a computed tomography (CT) scan at Teluk Intan Hospital early this morning as part of efforts to assess her condition. However, she still requires close monitoring and several additional examinations to ensure her recovery progresses well,” he told Bernama when contacted today.

items.title: Wisma Putra: Anwar's visit to Japan to open new avenues of cooperation
items.summary: TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake an official visit to Japan from tomorrow until June 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government to strengthen strategic ties and explore new avenues of cooperation between the two countries. He will be accompanied by Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim, and senior government officials. Wisma Putra, in a statement, said the prime minister will be accorded an official welcoming ceremony, followed by a bilateral meeting with his counterpart, Sanae Takaichi, Prime Minister of Japan.

items.title: OpenAI plans ChatGPT 'superapp' overhaul ahead of listing
items.summary: Reuters could not immediately verify the report. OpenAI did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment. ChatGPT serves more than 900 million weekly active users, OpenAI said earlier this year, adding that it had surpassed 50 million consumer subscribers.

items.title: IATA sees no near-term airline consolidation despite fuel price pressures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) does not expect a wave of airline consolidation in Asia Pacific in the near term despite rising fuel costs. IATA regional vice president for Asia Pacific Sheldon Hee said airlines in the region are particularly vulnerable to fuel price volatility. Asia Pacific carriers are generally more dependent on fuel and are therefore more affected by fuel price volatility."

items.title: Housing Ministry allocates RM267mil to boost Fire and Rescue Department readiness
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: The Housing and Local Government Ministry has allocated substantial funding to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department remains fully equipped and on constant standby for emergencies. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue operations. Nga said the ministry would provide written details on the estimated cost of the search and rescue operation.

items.title: Asia Pacific to drive 41pct of global air travel growth but challenges remain: IATA
items.summary: But infrastructure constraints, taxation and increasing regulatory burdens could undermine the region's long-term potential, the International Air Transport Association (IATA) said. IATA regional vice president for Asia Pacific Sheldon Hee said the region is projected to add 2.4 billion passengers between 2024 and 2044 with passenger numbers rising from 1.7 billion to 4.1 billion over the period. Despite the strong demand outlook, Hee said infrastructure, taxation, over-regulation and sustainable growth as the key challenges facing the region.

items.title: Recognition for Orang Asli in SAR reflects value of indigenous knowledge
items.summary: TAPAH: The appreciation and assistance extended by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi to the Orang Asli families involved in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing climber Jaslinda Saludin has been described as meaningful recognition of the community's contributions and capabilities in supporting emergency response efforts. Special Officer to the Perak Menteri Besar for Orang Asli Community Affairs Chew Ho Beng said the gesture highlighted the importance of the Orang Asli community's deep knowledge of forests and remote terrain, particularly in operations involving challenging environments. Chew also thanked the government for its concern for the families of Orang Asli volunteers who took part in the operation by providing assistance to them through the Department of Orang Asli Development (Jakoa).

items.title: South Korea vows to take stern action against speculative won trading
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korean Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol on Sunday vowed stern action against speculative and illegal currency trading, as the authorities scramble to curb the won's sharp decline. He said financial authorities will not tolerate the won's extreme volatility and one-sided moves caused by speculative trading. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: No one bigger than DAP, says Loke on impact of Marina's exit
items.summary: "DAP is a party of 60 years. The party has always prevailed," he said. Loke said party leaders and elected representatives came and went, but the party itself endured.

items.title: Anwar says leadership requires patience as he defends stance amid UEC criticism
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has stressed that community leaders must have a high level of patience in facing current challenges, while remaining committed alongside the Madani Government to realise the national development agenda. He said that despite various accusations, it is the responsibility of leaders to continue fulfilling the trust given by the people. Also present were Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

items.title: [UPDATED] Six, including baby, killed in Sungai Petani crash
items.summary: The deceased were identified as Proton X50 driver Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 27, Nora Mhd Husin, 55, Jamaliah Sannusi, 27, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, a 7-year-old boy and a 6-month-old baby. Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq, 3, suffered serious injuries in the 3.46pm crash. Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station chief Fauzi Shuaib said personnel from the Tikam Batu and Sungai Petani stations were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call.

items.title: 31 jellyfish sting cases recorded at Besut beach since May
items.summary: JERTIH: The Civil Defence Force in Besut has recorded 31 venomous jellyfish sting cases involving victims aged between three and 44 at Pantai Bukit Keluang from May until Thursday. Besut Civil Defence officer Captain Mohd Fazlien Jaafar said all the victims were visitors who had been swimming at the popular beach. Meanwhile, Fazlien said victims who sought assistance at the APM observation tower were given immediate treatment by personnel on duty.

items.title: Health Ministry targets RM1.8bil health infrastructure boost for Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA: The Health Ministry plans to develop several health infrastructure projects in Melaka worth RM1.8 billion under the Rolling Plan 2 of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP). Dzulkefly said the ministry also planned to demolish and rebuild the 64-year-old Jasin Health Clinic (Type 2) and Jasin District Integrated Health Complex at an estimated cost of RM126.99 million. The six facilities are the Durian Tunggal Health Clinic, Jasin Health Clinic, Jasin Hospital, Bukit Katil Rural Clinic, Melaka Hospital and Alor Gajah Hospital.

items.title: Goku, Luffy bring anime magic to NSTP cosplay contest at KL Book Fair
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: New Straits Times Press brought the virtual world to life this weekend by hosting an online cosplay competition in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair. Azwan, who won RM300, said he discovered the competition through NSTP's Facebook page and decided to participate because of his passion for cosplay. I enjoy seeing children happy when they meet their favourite characters," he said.

items.title: Missing US tourist on Pulau Tioman rescued after sharing real-time location
items.summary: KUANTAN: An American tourist reported missing while hiking on Pulau Tioman was found safe last night after sharing his real-time location via mobile phone with rescue teams. Rompin district police chief Superintendent Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said police received information about the missing man at 5.30pm yesterday after he failed to return to his resort following a hike along the old Kampung Tekek-Kampung Juara trail. "The victim was found at 8.33pm in the Bukit Parang forest area, Kampung Tekek, safe and without injuries.

items.title: Border security drive sees 91 illegal jetties demolished
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A total of 91 illegal jetties built on government land along the Malaysia-Thailand border have been demolished as part of ongoing efforts to curb cross-border crime and smuggling activities. "All 91 illegal jetties that we have demolished were built on government land," he told reporters after attending a border security-related meeting here today. Border authorities have identified 213 illegal jetties along the border, built on both government and private land.

items.title: [UPDATED] Unity above all, reject divisive racial politics, says PM
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to reject divisive racial narratives and strengthen unity for national stability and progress. "Malaysia's strength lies in cooperation among the Malays, Chinese, Indians and other communities. "Political stability has underpinned Malaysia's economic resilience, including currency strength and investor confidence, which depend on continued national unity," Anwar said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Bersatu vows to preserve unity with Pas in PN
items.summary: New Straits Times KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has reiterated its commitment to maintaining good relations with Pas within the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, amid speculation over tensions between the two parties. The development comes amid renewed tensions between the two parties, after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang reportedly said the party would re-evaluate its cooperation with Bersatu following several actions seen as going against the spirit of unity within PN. Hadi said Pas would make a decision on its political cooperation with Bersatu tomorrow.

items.title: Explainer: What to do when a parking tout tries to charge you a fee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Many Malaysians have encountered parking touts demanding payment for parking in public areas, often leaving motorists uncertain about whether such fees are legitimate. Parking touts are those who illegally or unofficially collect parking fees from drivers despite having no legal authority to do so. Although enforcement agencies have conducted numerous operations against these individuals, questions persist over whether parking touts have any right to collect fees in public spaces.

items.title: Endangered hawksbill turtle rescued from drain near Melaka motel
items.summary: MELAKA: A hawksbill turtle trapped in a drain near a motel in Tanjung Kling was rescued by the Fire and Rescue Department in an operation today. "At first, my workers saw the turtle in the drain near the motel. Concerned for the animal's safety, Tan said he then contacted the fire department for assistance.

items.title: Anwar rejects claims he is delaying elections to stay in office
items.summary: Instead, he said he wants to fully focus on restoring the country's economy and resolving issues affecting the people. "I want to focus on solving the people's problems first. Politics must be stable.

items.title: You can’t park there — Woman crashes Porsche into Gurney Drive hotel after family visit
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 7 — An elderly woman accidentally stepped on the accelerator, causing the Porsche she was driving to crash into the lobby area of a hotel at Persiaran Gurney here today, an incident that has also gone viral on social media. Northeast District Police Chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said that the police received a report regarding the incident from the 61-year-old local woman at 12:05 pm and investigations revealed that the accident occurred at around 11:15 am. Earlier, several photos went viral showing a grey Porsche inside a hotel lobby, believed to have lost control before crashing into the area.

items.title: MACC uncovers RM9mil false claims in Daya Kerjaya programme
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has uncovered irregularities involving the government's Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said investigations showed that 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide were linked to the alleged misuse of government funds under the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) initiative in 2024 and 2025. Abd Halim said the commission's Governance Investigation division would intensify efforts to assist government departments, agencies and companies in improving governance and strengthening procedures to prevent leakages and misuse of public funds.

items.title: PM rules out fuel price hike, says subsidies shield rakyat
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ruled out any increase in fuel prices, saying the government is already absorbing billions of ringgit monthly to protect Malaysians from global oil shocks. He said Malaysia continues to maintain one of the lowest retail fuel prices globally at RM1.99 per litre, despite volatile international oil markets driven by geopolitical tensions. He said the government spends an estimated RM3 billion to RM5 billion every month on fuel-related subsidies, with costs spiking further during periods of global price surges.

items.title: Angler drowns in Serendah lake
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 50-year-old man drowned while fishing at a lake behind the Serendah Health Clinic today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Zone 6 chief Mustakim Rimon said the department received a distress call at 12.06pm. He said four firemen from the Kuala Kubu Baru Fire and Rescue station were dispatched to the scene, about 28km away.

items.title: Agrobank brings financing to market for hawkers, micro traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Agrobank has expanded its outreach to hawkers and micro-entrepreneurs by bringing its financing solutions directly to the Kelana Jaya farmers' market to improve access to funding for small businesses. The engagement session, attended by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, forms part of the ministry's initiative aimed at strengthening engagement with small traders and better understanding the challenges faced by the micro-enterprise community. Agrobank's participation was also in line with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's call for financial institutions to engage directly with small businesses and help them secure the capital needed to start, sustain and expand their operations.

items.title: ‘Please stay in one place’: Rescued hiker Jaslinda found messages meant to save her, but days too late
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — The female climber who was found safe after going missing in the Gunung Batu Putih area, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found two notes left by the search and rescue (SAR) team at Kem Air Busok during the operation. “The note was left by a forestry mountain guide (MGP) known as Hafiz while conducting a search in the Kem Air Busok area on May 30. “On that day, the search team focused on the route from Pos Musoh to Kem Air Busok and the note was left as an indication that the SAR team had passed through the area,” he said.

items.title: Rescued hiker Jaslinda in stable condition, recovering
items.summary: TAPAH: The condition of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who is receiving treatment at Tapah Hospital, is reported to be improving. However, she said Jaslinda remains in stable condition, although she still requires further medical treatment and close monitoring. "Jaslinda is currently stable.

items.title: Police target influencers, online syndicates in World Cup gambling crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have warned social media influencers against promoting illegal gambling activities as authorities brace for an anticipated surge in online betting linked to the upcoming FIFA World Cup. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said police intelligence indicated that almost 90 per cent of illegal betting activities during the tournament would take place online, with syndicates increasingly relying on digital platforms, messaging applications and social media to recruit customers. "Police are aware of the trend of social media influencers being used to promote illegal gambling activities on social media platforms," he told the New Straits Times.

items.title: No MyKad visible in viral video of alleged Myanmar national, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have found that no identity document or MyKad was shown in a viral video alleging that a Myanmar national possessed a Malaysian identity card. Putrajaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Aidi Sham Mohamed said investigations revealed that the video circulating on social media did not contain any visual evidence to support claims that the individual featured in the recording was holding a MyKad. Initial investigations found that the video had been shared on Facebook through the account "Na'am Ahmad", questioning how an individual purportedly from Myanmar could possess a blue MyKad.

items.title: 'Our prayers have been answered': Jaslinda's daughter on mum's rescue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: One of Jaslinda Saludin's daughters has described her mother's rescue as an answered prayer, thanking Malaysians and search personnel who never gave up hope during the search for the missing hiker. In a post on Threads, Dhia Damia Mohd Faizal said she was grateful that her mother was found yesterday, two weeks after she went missing during a hike on Gunung Batu Putih. Praise be to Allah.

items.title: Upko’s application to join Sabah ruling coalition set for decision on June 18
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 7 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) is expected to decide on the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation's (Upko) application to join the coalition as a component party on June 18. He said a key condition for Upko's admission is its commitment to GRS’ four core principles: Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United. GRS currently comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and Parti Cinta Sabah.

items.title: Govt approves RM267m to keep Bomba on 100pc standby ‘at all times’ for emergencies
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 7 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) has allocated substantial funds to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) remains fully prepared for emergencies at all times. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue missions. Nga said the ministry will provide written details on the estimated costs of Jaslinda’s search and rescue mission.

items.title: Former Kedah deputy speaker shared final dinner after daughter's engagement
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Nur Anisa Mohd Jamil's last memory of her father was sharing dinner after her engagement ceremony, a day before he was found drowned today. Nur Anisa, 27, the youngest child of Mohd Jamil, said her father appeared healthy and in good spirits during her engagement ceremony yesterday. Mohd Jamil, 78, a former Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman, was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan in Tandop today.

items.title: Jaslinda found note left by mountain guide during search for her
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found a note left behind by rescuers at Kem Air Busuk on Gunung Batu Putih while search and rescue (SAR) operations were underway. Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said Jaslinda brought the note out with her and showed it to firefighters yesterday after she was rescued. "Jaslinda only reached the area yesterday," he said.

items.title: The highway crawl begins: Slow afternoon traffic as holidaymakers return to the Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Traffic flow on several major highways leading to Kuala Lumpur was reported to be slow as of 2pm today as motorists began returning home on the final day of the mid-year school holidays and following recent double public holidays. Meanwhile, on the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway (KLK) heading towards Kuala Lumpur, slow-moving traffic was detected from Gambang to Sri Jaya and along the stretch between Lentang and Bukit Tinggi. As a proactive measure to ease congestion and improve traffic flow, highway authorities have activated Smart Lanes, which utilise emergency lanes at several strategic locations.

items.title: Perikatan on the brink? PAS to decide fate of Bersatu alliance in Monday meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The future of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition hangs in the balance as PAS will convene a high-stakes central working committee (CWC) meeting tomorrow night to determine its ongoing relationship with Bersatu. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed the meeting today, when speaking to reporters following the launch of a book on the Iran-Israel war at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair, Free Malaysia Today reported. We will have a meeting at night,” Hadi said, addressing mounting speculation over when the party leadership would formally decide the trajectory of the PAS-Bersatu partnership.

items.title: ‘UPSI Berduka’: Book, fund and new crisis management guide follow Gerik bus crash that killed 15 varsity students
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, June 7 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) has published a book titled UPSI Berduka , documenting the tragic road accident involving its students in Gerik, Perak, as a reference and guide for future crisis management and emergency response efforts. UPSI vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff said the publication, which took nearly 10 months to complete, details the chronology of the tragedy, the seven phases of crisis management undertaken by the university in collaboration with various agencies, and personal accounts from those directly affected by the incident. He said the book serves as a comprehensive record of the experiences of victims’ families, survivors, volunteers, rescue personnel, frontline workers and other parties involved throughout the management of the tragedy.

items.title: Outgoing J-Kom chief Ismail Yusop thanks staff, says new responsibility awaits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Department of Community Communications (J-Kom) Director-General Datuk Ismail Yusop has announced the end of his service after more than a year leading the department, saying he will soon take on a new assignment. In a Facebook post, Ismail, affectionately known as “Tok Abah”, said every journey in life has its moments of meeting and parting, and that every ending opens the door to a new beginning. Mohd Khairuddin, also Selangor PKR secretary, was appointed J-Kom Director-General on March 1, 2023, replacing Prof Datuk Dr Mohammad Agus Yusof.

items.title: Anwar greenlights RM25m annual grant for AIMST, plus RM1m top-up for needy students
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — The federal government has approved an additional RM1 million allocation for AIMST University to ensure students from underprivileged families continue to receive education assistance. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the additional allocation would supplement the annual grant given to the private university. Also present were Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir and MIC president Tan Sri Dr SA Vigneswaran, who is also AIMST University chancellor and chairman.At the ceremony, AIMST University received an annual grant amounting to RM25 million.

items.title: RM205m lifeline for MSMEs as Putrajaya unveils new business support initiatives
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development (Kuskop), through SME Corp Malaysia, has launched an industry and business support programme (Sinar) to assist micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Its minister, Steven Sim Chee Keong, said the Sinar programme offers financial assistance totalling RM205 million through four new initiatives for MSMEs nationwide to help expand their businesses. Elaborating further, he said Sinar is a new Kuskop programme implemented through SME Corp Malaysia, encompassing five new initiatives involving financial assistance.

items.title: Govt to scale up food rescue efforts under Ihsan Food Bank programme
items.summary: Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the initiative would serve as a guide for all stakeholders involved in carrying out the programme more effectively and systematically. “We intend to strengthen the i-FB programme so that we can develop and publish an action plan that will serve as a reference for all parties involved in implementing the initiative,” he told reporters after officiating the Ihsan Food Bank (i-FB) Initiative Programme here today. Armizan said the action plan would help align the direction and objectives of the programme while improving coordination among participating organisations and agencies.

items.title: ‘I can safely say 17 seats’ — Anthony Loke confirms DAP’s Johor allocation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — DAP is set to expand its footprint in the upcoming Johor state election, fielding candidates in 17 seats, an increase from the 14 it contested in the 2022 polls. “I can safely say that DAP will be contesting 17 seats in the coming state election in Johor,” Loke was quoted as saying in a Free Malaysia Today report. In the 2022 state election, DAP emerged as the coalition’s strongest performer in Johor, successfully defending 10 seats.

items.title: After 50 years of floods, govt plans RM80.5m project to make Teluk Intan flood-free by 2028
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 7 — Teluk Intan is expected to be declared a flood-free zone by 2028 through the implementation of the Phase Two Teluk Intan Flood Mitigation Plan (RTB), also known as the Teluk Intan Smart RTB project, which is currently in progress. “In less than 30 months, we will witness a new face of Teluk Intan. During the event, Nga also launched the Teluk Intan RTB Zone A Phase Two project, which commenced in December last year.

items.title: Anwar to address Nikkei Forum, explore new investments in three-day visit to Japan
items.summary: TOKYO, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake an official visit to Japan from June 8 to 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government, according to the Foreign Affairs Ministry. He will be accompanied by Foreign Affairs Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong and senior government officials. “The Prime Minister will be accorded an official welcoming ceremony, followed by a bilateral meeting with his counterpart, Prime Minister of Japan Sanae Takaichi,” the ministry, widely known as Wisma Putra, said in a statement today.

items.title: Heavy rain, strong winds on the way: Thunderstorm warning in effect for Johor, Pahang, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several districts across Pahang, Johor, and Sarawak, cautioning residents to expect heavy rain, strong winds, and thunderstorms through 3 pm today. The warning, issued at 1:05 pm, covers the following areas: Pahang : Rompin Johor : Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai, and Kota Tinggi Sarawak : Sri Aman; Betong (Pusa, Saratok, and Kabong); Sarikei; Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu, and Dalat); and Miri MetMalaysia triggers a thunderstorm warning when rainfall intensity is forecast to exceed 20 millimetres per hour, either imminently or for a sustained period of more than one hour. The warning remains in effect until 3:00 pm.

items.title: Body of 64-year-old man found in Kampung Kolam drain near Kepala Batas, cops say no foul play
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, June 7 — The body of an elderly man was found in a drain at Kampung Kolam, Jalan Merbau Kudung here yesterday, with injuries to his left ankle. Seberang Perai Utara district police chief ACP Anuar Abdul Rahman said police received a report on the discovery of the 64-year-old man’s body from a man who was on his way to go fishing in the nearby area. “Investigations found that the victim was last seen by a witness about two days before the incident.

items.title: Johor MCA seeks temporary base after fire destroys 80pc of Yong Peng office
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — A fire that destroyed about 80 per cent of MCA’s office in Yong Peng yesterday has disrupted the party’s preparations for the upcoming Johor state election, its Youth chief Ling Tian Soon said. Ling said MCA is considering relocating its operations to a temporary premises after investigations by the Fire and Rescue Department are completed. The Yong Peng assemblyman said the blaze destroyed the building’s public address system, newly installed air-conditioning units, furniture, as well as documents and equipment used for community programmes.

items.title: ‘Johor has enormous potential’: Pakatan communications chief calls on poll campaigners to focus on development issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The upcoming Johor state election is not merely a political contest but an important platform to determine the future direction of the state and the well-being of its people, said Pakatan Harapan (PH) Communications Director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1 to pave the way for the state election. — Bernama Pakatan Harapan communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil (left) speaks to Johor election campaign workers in Batu Pahat on June 6, 2026 in preparation for the coming state election.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim grants audience to caretaker Johor menteri besar at Istana Pasir Pelangi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi at Istana Pasir Pelangi here. According to a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty received a briefing on Johor's economic performance, development and progress. The briefing also covered efforts undertaken by the Johor state government to enhance the well-being of the people.

items.title: Highways clear this morning, but LLM warns of heavier traffic as holidaymakers return to Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — All highways heading towards the Klang Valley were experiencing smooth traffic flow this morning. He also advised motorists to plan their travel times to avoid getting caught in traffic jams. — Bernama

items.title: From ‘critical stage’ to miracle: The full 14-day survival story of Jaslinda Saludin (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, June 7 — Woman hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing for 14 days after she went on a hike at Gunung Batu Putih, near Tapah was found safe near Kampung Lubuk Geharu, Pos Musoh today by Orang Asli villager Nazri a/l Bah Eng at about 5 pm. May 23 Jaslinda, with 13 other people and two forestry mountain guides start their Trans Spencer Chapman expedition at 2 am on the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route. A search and rescue operation carries on looking for Jaslinda with a team of 12 Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team (STORM) members dispatched to the peak of Gunung Batu Putih.

items.title: Former Kedah deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil found dead in river near Alor Setar after disappearing overnight
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 7 — Former Kedah State Assembly deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil Md Idross was found dead this morning after he was believed to have fallen into Sungai Teluk Bagan along Jalan Tandop here. Kota Setar police chief ACP Syed Basri Syed Ali said personnel from the Alor Setar Fire and Rescue Department discovered the 78-year-old’s body at about 8.10am. According to Syed Basri, family members last saw Mohd Jamil at about 11.30pm yesterday.

items.title: Pakatan completes Johor seat negotiations, shifts focus to election machinery preparation
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — Seat negotiations among component parties within Pakatan Harapan (PH) for the 16th Johor State Election have been finalised, says PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Thank you, Abang Mat Sabu (Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu), thank you Anthony Loke (DAP secretary-general),” he said while speaking at the launch of the PH election machinery for the Johor State Election, which was also attended by the Prime Minister and PH chairman, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that with the seat negotiations completed, PH’s focus would now shift to election machinery preparations, including on-the-ground work, logistics coordination and delivering the right messages to voters to ensure PH’s victory in Johor.

items.title: ‘Don't treat people like slaves’ — Anwar vows end to degrading aid distribution practices
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reaffirmed the MADANI Government’s commitment to ending outdated practices that undermine the dignity of the people, including forcing those in need to queue for long hours to receive financial aid. Also present were DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook, PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Also in attendance were PKR vice-president and Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa, Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan and Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching.

items.title: Mosti monitoring Sabah airspace after reports of mysterious ‘bright light’ sighting
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 7 — The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti) has initiated a monitoring operation following reports of a mysterious bright light sighted in Sabah’s airspace earlier this week. In a statement today, the ministry confirmed that its Space Regulatory Authority Division (BPAngkasa) has received preliminary information regarding the phenomenon, which was reportedly observed by members of the public on the night of June 4. “Active monitoring is currently being carried out by BPAngkasa in cooperation with relevant agencies,” the statement read.

items.title: The cost of a heartbeat: How CVSKL Foundation is helping Malaysia’s sickest heart patients
items.summary: CVSKL Foundation chairman Tan Sri Rashpal Singh Randhay said the foundation was established as part of a broader commitment to ensure that financial hardship does not become a barrier to lifesaving care. We wanted to help patients who genuinely have no means of obtaining the treatment they need,” he said. Rashpal said the foundation’s assessment process is designed to ensure limited resources are directed to patients with the greatest need.
